channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Over 100,000 people join protest rally in Belgrade against Serbia's president and government
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — At least 100,000 people descended on Belgrade on Saturday for a mass rally seen as a culmination of months-long protests against Serbia's populist President Aleksandar Vucic and his government.
item.dc:creator: JOVANA GEC and DUSAN STOJANOVIC, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A judge limits Trump's ability to deport people under the 18th century Alien Enemies Act
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration is appealing a judge's early order that it cannot immediately deport a number of Venezuelans migrants under an 18th century law called the Alien Enemies Act.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope works on signature reform from hospital as Vatican cuts back on updates on medical progress
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis entered the fifth week of hospital treatment for double pneumonia Saturday very much looking ahead as he worked on a signature priority of his papacy and signs of his recovery remained on a positive trajectory.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Freed Israeli hostage speaks for the first time about his 505 days of surviving Hamas hell
item.description: Freed hostage Tal Shoham shares his 505-day ordeal under Hamas, surviving abuse and psychological warfare while clinging to hope. His story is one of resilience, defiance and the will to return home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Starmer challenges Putin to prove he is serious about peace by signing up to Ukraine ceasefire
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Saturday challenged Russian President Vladimir Putin to sign up to a ceasefire in its war against Ukraine if he is serious about peace, and said allies will keep increasing the pressure on the Kremlin, including by moving planning for a peacekeepi
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Trump's pick for hostage negotiations envoy withdraws nomination but will continue on hostage issues
item.description: Adam Boehler, President Donald Trump’s nominee to become special envoy for hostage affairs, has withdrawn his nomination to avoid a requirement that he divest from his investment business.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Why Trump is singling out South Africa and accusing it of being anti-white and anti-American
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — The Trump administration’s decision to expel the South African ambassador is its latest move against a country it has singled out for sanctions and accused of being anti-white and anti-American. U.S.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russia and Ukraine trade overnight aerial attacks after Putin sets out conditions for ceasefire
item.description: Russia and Ukraine have traded heavy aerial blows with both sides reporting more than 100 enemy drones over their respective territories overnight.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rubio declares South African ambassador to US 'persona non grata' over Trump comments
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio declared South African ambassador to the U.S. Ebrahim Rasool “persona non grata” and accused the diplomat of being a “race-baiting politician” over his recent comments about President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Thousands converging on Serbia's capital for a major rally that tests Vucic's populist government
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Tens of thousands of people swarmed the streets in Serbia's capital Belgrade on Friday, staging a joyful prelude to a major anti-government rally on the weekend and dealing a blow to populist President Aleksandar Vucic.
item.dc:creator: JOVANA GEC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mark Carney is sworn in as Canada's new prime minister as country deals with Trump's trade war
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Former central banker Mark Carney was sworn in as Canada’s new prime minister on Friday, and will now try to steer his country through a trade war brought by U.S. President Donald Trump, annexation threats and an expected federal election.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Greenland political parties join together against Trump comments about annexing the island
item.description: LONDON (AP) — All five parties in Greenland’s parliament issued a joint statement on Friday rejecting President Donald Trump’s latest effort to take control of the strategic Arctic island.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philippine ex-President Duterte appears by video link at International Criminal Court hearing
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — Jailed former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte appeared Friday by videoconference before judges at the International Criminal Court, days after his arrest in Manila on murder charges linked to the deadly “ war on drugs ” that he oversaw while in office.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CORDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas says it will release a US-Israeli hostage and 4 bodies, but Israel expresses immediate doubt
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Palestinian militant group Hamas said Friday that it has accepted a proposal from mediators to release one living American-Israeli hostage and the bodies of four dual nationals who had died in captivity.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Whipsawed by Trump's tariffs, the US public is getting a lot more nervous about the economy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's volatile tariff threats are unleashing historic jumps in public anxiety, with the potential to undermine his pledges to strengthen a U.S. economy that is increasingly weakened.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's Irish golf course damaged by pro-Palestinian activists
item.description: The incident comes less than a week after pro-Palestinian protestors dug up some of the Trump Turnberry greens in Scotland.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he's asked Putin to spare Ukrainian troops' lives
item.description: President Trump said he strongly requested that Russian President Vladimir Putin spare the lives of Ukrainian soldiers surrounded by Russian troops, in a statement posted to his social media site. Trump described “good and productive” discussions between U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Global alert issued for missing college student Sudiksha Konanki
item.description: Sudiksha Konanki is one of six female University of Pittsburgh students who traveled together to the Dominican Republic for spring break.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: American draws Australian PM's ire after snatching wombat
item.description: Australian immigration officials could bar an American influencer from ever returning Down Under for carrying a baby wombat away from its mother.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Dozens of Syrian Druze make a rare visit to Israeli-controlled Golan Heights
item.description: MAJDAL SHAMS, Golan Heights (AP) — Dozens of clerics and others from Syria’s minority Druze community crossed into the Israeli-controlled side of the Golan Heights Friday for the first time in decades.
item.dc:creator: BY ALON BERNSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ontario Premier Ford hails 'positive' meeting with Lutnick
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford lauded his Thursday meeting with Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick as “positive” and “productive” after their public rift over tariffs on imported goods.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Putin says he agrees in principle with US proposal for 30-day ceasefire in Ukraine
item.description: “So the idea itself is correct, and we certainly support it,” Putin told a news conference in Moscow.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Poland calls on US to place nukes within its borders amid Russia threat
item.description: Polish President Duda has once again called on the US to place its nuclear arms within its borders to better deter Russia, mirroring talks with France amid security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump threatens 200% tariff on European wine after proposed whiskey tariff
item.description: Trump vowed a new escalation in his trade war if the EU pushes ahead with the planned 50% tariff on American whiskey.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russia presents US with demands that need to be met before ending Ukraine war: report
item.description: Russia has reportedly laid out its demands for ending the war in Ukraine and they mirror previous requests made to Kyiv, the U.S. and NATO.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pakistan says insurgents who attacked a passenger train killed 21 hostages while all others freed
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Insurgents who attacked a passenger train carrying 440 passengers in restive southwestern Pakistan killed 21 hostages before security forces killed all 33 of the assailants, and all other passengers were rescued, officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says tariffs will be 'positive'; Vance pushes Russia for ceasefire
item.description: “The United States of America is going to take back a lot of what was stolen from it by other countries.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Greenland's center-right party pulls off upset victory as Trump seeks control
item.description: Greenland's center-right Demokraatit party saw a sharp increase in support as it won the country's election on Tuesday. Independence became a central issue as President Donald Trump talked of a U.S. takeover.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Detained captain of cargo ship that collided with US oil tanker is Russian national, ship owner says
item.description: A Russian national was revealed to be the captain of the cargo ship that collided with an American tanker off the coast of England, spilling U.S. military jet fuel into the sea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel kills 8 Palestinians in Gaza as Hamas says ceasefire talks resume
item.description: Israeli fire has killed eight Palestinians in the Gaza Strip and three more in the occupied West Bank over the past 24 hours, Palestinian officials said Tuesday, even as a fragile ceasefire with Hamas in Gaza has largely held since late January.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US-Canada products are 99% tariff-free: Canadian ambassador to US
item.description: Canada's ambassador to the United States said 99% of products traded between the two nations are tariff-free.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Doctors declare Pope Francis no longer in imminent danger due to pneumonia, but remains hospitalized
item.description: ROME (AP) — Doctors said Monday Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia that has kept him hospitalized for nearly a month, but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Doctors declare Pope no longer in imminent danger, but remains hospitalized
item.description: Doctors said Monday that Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Syria's worst violence in months reopens wounds of the civil war
item.description: An ambush on a Syrian security patrol by gunmen loyal to ousted leader Bashar Assad escalated into clashes that a war monitor estimates have killed more than 1,000 people over four days.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pro-Palestinian activists vandalize Trump golf course in Scotland
item.description: In white bold-face capital letters, activists wrote “Gaza is not 4 sale” on Trump’s Turnberry golf course.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation
